title: eriogonum heermannii subsp. sulcatum
intro: Eriogonum heermannii subsp. sulcatum, also known as the Sulcate Buckwheat or Sulcate Mormon Tea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buckwheat family, Polygonaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Eriogonum heermannii subsp. sulcatum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to promote overall well-being and support immune function.
